垮.origin: 土 earth plus 夸; when a wall of earth falls down and collapses.

sentence.en: That wall collapsed.

埃.origin: 土 earth plus 矣; tiny bits of earth floating as dust.

sentence.en: There is a layer of dust on the table.

埝.origin: 土 earth plus 念; a small earthen dike that holds water.

sentence.en: A dike was built beside the field.

埯.origin: 土 earth plus 奄; a small pit dug in the soil for planting.

word.en: to sow seeds in small pits

sentence.en: He digs small pits in the field.

堂.origin: 土 earth plus 尚; a big building with a wide floor, a hall.

sentence.en: It is very quiet in the classroom.

堆.origin: 土 earth plus 隹; a pile of earth or things stacked up.

sentence.en: There is a pile of sand on the ground.

堕.origin: 土 earth below; to drop down and fall.

sentence.en: The stone falls into the river.

堡.origin: Earthen 土 walls built to protect 保 people, a fort.

sentence.en: There is a castle on the hill.

堤.origin: 土 earth plus 是; a wall of earth that holds back river water.

sentence.en: We stroll along the riverbank.

堰.origin: 土 earth plus 匽; a low earthen dam that stores water.

sentence.en: There is a dammed pond in the village.

堵.origin: 土 earth plus 者; to block something up, like a wall of earth.

sentence.en: There is a traffic jam on the road.

塌.origin: 土 earth plus 日羽; when something made of earth falls in.

sentence.en: The old house collapsed.

塑.origin: 土 earth plus 朔; to shape soft clay into a model.

sentence.en: This cup is made of plastic.

塔.origin: 土 earth plus 荅; a tall building that rises up high.

sentence.en: There is a tower in the distance.

塘.origin: 土 earth plus 唐; an earthen bank holding a pool of water.

sentence.en: There are little fish in the pond.

塞.origin: 土 earth at the bottom; to stuff or block something up.

sentence.en: He stoppered the bottle.

填.origin: 土 earth plus 真; to put earth in until a hole is full.

sentence.en: Please fill in your name.

塬.origin: 土 earth plus 原; a high, flat tableland of earth.

sentence.en: Crops are grown on the plateau.

墓.origin: 土 earth plus 莫; a grave made in the earth.

sentence.en: There are a few graves at the foot of the hill.

增.origin: 土 earth plus 曾; piling more earth on, to add and increase.

sentence.en: The weather grows warmer day by day.

墨.origin: Black 黑 powder from earth 土 makes ink.

sentence.en: The pen has run out of ink.

墩.origin: 土 earth plus 敦; a solid block of earth or stone.

sentence.en: The bridge piers stand in the river.

壁.origin: 土 earth plus 辟; a wall made of earth or stone.

sentence.en: A picture hangs on the wall.

壕.origin: 土 earth plus 豪; a ditch dug into the ground.

sentence.en: There is a trench outside the city.

士.origin: A person who studies and serves, a scholar or gentleman.

sentence.en: The soldier is very brave.

壬.origin: The ninth of the ten heavenly stems used in old counting.

word.en: renwu (a year in the old calendar)

sentence.en: This year is a ren year.

壮.origin: 丬 plus 士; a strong, sturdy person, full of power.

sentence.en: He is built strong.

壳.origin: A hard outer cover; the shell of an egg or a snail.

sentence.en: There are many seashells on the beach.

壶.origin: A picture of a jar or pot used to hold water or wine.

sentence.en: There is hot water in the teapot.

壹.origin: The formal way to write one, used on bank notes and checks.

sentence.en: This is one yuan.

处.origin: To go 夂 somewhere and stay; a place where you live.

sentence.en: There are flowers everywhere in the park.

备.origin: To go 夂 to the field 田 and get things ready for harvest.

sentence.en: I am ready.

夕.origin: A picture of a crescent moon; it means evening.

sentence.en: The setting sun is beautiful.

夯.origin: 大 big plus 力 strength; using great strength to pound earth.

sentence.en: The workers are tamping the ground.

失.origin: Something slipping 丿 out of a hand 夫; you lose it.

sentence.en: I lost a pen.

夸.origin: 大 big plus 亏; to say big words, to praise or boast.

sentence.en: The teacher praises me.

夹.origin: A person 夫 squeezed between two others 丷; to hold between.

sentence.en: Hold the paper with a clip.

夺.origin: A hand 寸 of a big person 大 grabbing; to snatch away.

sentence.en: He snatched away my ball.

奉.origin: Two hands holding something up to offer with respect.

sentence.en: He devotes himself to everyone.

奏.origin: Two hands offering up music; to play an instrument.

sentence.en: She is playing the piano.

奔.origin: A person 大 running fast through a field of grass 卉.

sentence.en: The horse runs across the grassland.

奕.origin: 亦 plus 大; it means grand and full of life and spirit.

sentence.en: He is full of energy.

奖.origin: Something good given to a great 大 deed; a prize.

sentence.en: I won a prize.

套.origin: A big 大 person wrapped in a long cloth; a cover or case.

sentence.en: You should wear gloves in winter.

奚.origin: An old question word meaning where, what, or why.

sentence.en: Don't mock others.

奥.origin: A person 大 finding a house 宀 full of rice 米; deep and mysterious.

sentence.en: The sea hides many mysteries.

奴.origin: A woman 女 working by a master's hand 又; a servant.

sentence.en: In ancient times there were slaves.

奸.origin: 女 woman plus 干; it means crafty or dishonest.

sentence.en: This person is very cunning.

妄.origin: 亡 plus 女; it means foolish, reckless, or absurd.

sentence.en: He is too arrogant.

妇.origin: A woman 女 with a broom 彐; it means a married woman.

sentence.en: The women are dancing.
